entries.title: Ephraim the Syrian on Genesis 44
entries.excerpt:
Joseph’s brothers ate of the meal, drank, rested, and set out on their way. In Benjamin’s sack the cup had been placed, and in each man’s sack his silver. After them Joseph’s steward is sent in pursuit, overtakes them, and strikes their ears with threats, as his master had commanded him.

They, confident of their innocence, say: “With whomsoever it be found, let him die, and we also will be my lord’s bondmen” (Gen 44:9). Then they hastily open their sacks.…
